Corrections.CorrectedClaim: 2 Command Injection vulnerabilities have been confirmed via source code audit (one via Configuration Validation, one via Native Admin Execution). Both follow the general pattern defined by SC-V3-004.

Items.Key: arbitrary local command execution - admin command interface|command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|606|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|upon receiving an 'exec' command, the function constructs a string slice: line 606 `std::string cmd = command.substr(5);`. it then executes this string argument directly via line 608 `system(cmd.c_str());`. an attacker can bypass the cli input with arguments like `4123 ; cat /etc/shadow` to include shell metacharacters.|complete internal system compromise, allowing attacker to run any command with the elevated privileges of the supercalc application.|the standard `admin exec` interface, passing a string containing shell control characters (`&`, `|`, `;`, `$()`).|strictly forbid the use of `system()`. use `fork`/`exec` sequence api calls where arguments are supplied as array pointers, ensuring they never reach the shell interpreter, and perform strict parameter validation and sanitization.

truthAudit.Summary: The auditors successfully verified the presence of all initially reported critical vulnerabilities identified in Run 2, confirming the existence of Input Buffer Overflows and two distinct instances of Command Injection within the source code provided. No false positives were identified from the previously claimed vulnerabilities, though many unrelated structural vulnerabilities were omitted.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote:
Directly referencing Line 567 in the evidence: line_start: 564, line_end: 567
Evidence: Line 566: `std::string command = validator_cmd + " " + config_file;` followed by Line 567: `int result = system(command.c_str());`... allows high-privilege arbitrary command execution (same privilege as the SuperCalc process).
Items.Notes: The configuration validation logic uses user/settings controlled `validator_cmd` and `config_file` in an un-safe format string which generates OS system calls with combined arguments, aligning exactly with SC-V3-004's specification including the relevant system call anchor sequence (validator_cmd + " " + config_file; system(command.c_str()); admin validate). This exposure was confirmed correctly in Run 2.

The buffer handling logic in handle_variable_command is flawed. As noted by Run 2, lines 831-832 exhibit a bounds check failure pathway that falls into strcpy(), copying attacker-controlled 'expression' data into 'input_buffer_' (L0664) without proper boundary enforcement if the logic branch takes the unsafe path (Although the condition in Line 0831 is supposed to intercept the length, the explicit logic in line 0832 remains dangerously vulnerable to larger inputs than accounted for in simple variable substitution analysis). This confirms the vulnerability SC-V3-005.
